card.intuition: A structural pattern that allows objects with incompatible interfaces to collaborate by converting the interface of one class into an interface expected by the client.
card.analogy: A travel plug: your hair dryer has a 3-prong UK plug (**Class A**), but the wall socket is a 2-prong European hole (**Client expecting Class B**). You put an 'Adapter' in the middle that 'speaks' both languages.
when_to_use: Integrating a third-party library that uses different method names than your existing code
Converting legacy XML data into JSON for a modern frontend component
Refactoring code where you want to swap a library for a better one without changing your core logic
gotchas: Don't confuse it with 'Bridge' or 'Proxy'. Adapter changes the interface; Proxy keeps the same interface; Bridge separates interface from implementation.
Overuse: if you have 50 adapters, it's likely a sign that your core architecture is messy.

exercise.prompt: Identify the primary goal of the Adapter pattern.
